name: Clovis Comet
description: The Clovis Comet hypothesis posits that a large comet or multiple cometary fragments impacted North America around 12,900 years ago, triggering a Younger Dryas impact event. This event is claimed to have caused widespread wildfires, megafauna extinctions, and the abrupt end of the Clovis culture, the earliest widespread archaeological culture in North America. Proponents argue that this event initiated a period of rapid cooling and environmental change. Evidence cited includes a 'black mat' layer found in archaeological sites, supposedly containing high concentrations of iridium, nanodiamonds, microspherules, and charcoal. The hypothesis originated in the late 2000s, gaining traction through publications by Richard Firestone, Allen West, and others. Mainstream scientific consensus, however, remains highly skeptical. Critics argue that the evidence is often misinterpreted, contaminated, or lacks consistent corroboration across different sites. Alternative explanations for the Younger Dryas cooling, such as changes in ocean currents or volcanic activity, are generally favored by mainstream researchers. The debate continues with ongoing research and re-evaluation of the evidence.
subtitle: Cometary impact and Clovis extinction theory

claims.claim: A comet impact caused the Younger Dryas cooling.
claims.evidence: Elevated levels of iridium, nanodiamonds, microspherules in the Younger Dryas Boundary layer.
claims.criticism: Iridium and nanodiamond concentrations are often not consistently elevated or are explained by terrestrial sources or contamination. Other explanations for the Younger Dryas cooling are more parsimonious.

claims.claim: The Clovis culture was destroyed by the comet impact.
claims.evidence: The disappearance of Clovis artifacts coincides with the Younger Dryas Boundary layer.
claims.criticism: The Clovis culture's decline may have been gradual and driven by various factors, including overhunting, climate change, and adaptation to new environments. Correlation does not equal causation.

claims.claim: Widespread wildfires were caused by the impact.
claims.evidence: High concentrations of charcoal in the Younger Dryas Boundary layer.
claims.criticism: Charcoal deposits may be due to natural wildfires, human activity, or other causes unrelated to a comet impact. The extent and intensity of wildfires are debated.

keyFigures.name: Richard Firestone
keyFigures.role: Proponent and co-author of 'The Cycle of Cosmic Catastrophes'

keyFigures.name: Allen West
keyFigures.role: Proponent and co-author of 'The Cycle of Cosmic Catastrophes'

keyFigures.name: Todd A. Surovell
keyFigures.role: Archaeologist and critic of the Clovis Comet hypothesis

timeline.event: Proposed comet impact and Younger Dryas Boundary
Emergence of the Clovis Comet hypothesis
Continued debate and research on the Younger Dryas and Clovis extinction

sources.author: Firestone, R. B., West, A., & Warwick-Smith, S.
sources.work: The Cycle of Cosmic Catastrophes: How a Stone-Age Comet Changed the Course of World Culture
sources.title: Firestone, R. B., West, A., & Warwick-Smith, S.

worshipPractices.name: Comet Vigil
worshipPractices.description: Individuals gather on the anniversary of the purported impact to observe the night sky, seeking omens or signs related to future cosmic events. They often share stories of the Clovis people and contemplate the fragility of civilization in the face of celestial threats.

worshipPractices.name: Ashes Ritual
worshipPractices.description: Participants collect and burn symbolic representations of the extinct megafauna and Clovis tools, scattering the ashes as a reminder of the destructive power of the comet. This act is intended to promote humility and respect for the natural world.

festivals.name: The Burning Sky Festival
festivals.description: A festival held to commemorate the theorized impact event. Includes bonfires, storytelling, and artistic representations of the comet's arrival and its consequences, symbolizing both destruction and renewal.

festivals.name: The Clovis Remembrance
festivals.timing: Anniversary of the proposed impact (varied based on interpretation)
festivals.description: A somber occasion marked by quiet reflection and contemplation of the lost Clovis culture. Participants often create temporary art installations using earth and natural materials to honor the past.

cultural.modernLegacy: The Clovis Comet theory has inspired numerous science fiction works, exploring themes of existential threats and societal collapse. It also fuels ongoing debates about catastrophic events and their impact on human history, often appearing in documentaries and speculative history programs.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Ragnarok involves a cataclysmic event, including fire and destruction, leading to the end of an era and the death of many beings, mirroring the Clovis Comet's supposed impact and subsequent extinctions.

Deucalion's flood was a destructive event sent by Zeus to punish humanity, resulting in widespread destruction and a new beginning, similar to the Clovis Comet's hypothesized role in reshaping North American ecosystems and cultures.

The Great Flood, sent by the gods, wiped out most of humanity. Like the Clovis Comet hypothesis, it represents a catastrophic event causing widespread devastation and a near-total reset of civilization.

Pralaya signifies a cosmic dissolution or deluge that periodically destroys the universe, leading to a period of quiescence before a new creation. This cyclical destruction resonates with the Clovis Comet theory as a potential trigger for large scale extinction and alteration of the world.

associations.name: Ragnarok Star
associations.significance: In Norse mythology, Ragnarok is heralded by celestial disturbances; the Clovis Comet could be seen as a similar harbinger of destruction and societal collapse.

associations.name: Fire Serpent
associations.significance: The comet's impact caused widespread wildfires; the fire serpent symbolizes the destructive power and transformative nature of fire.

associations.name: Extinct Megafauna
associations.significance: The comet is associated with the extinction of megafauna such as mammoths, representing the loss of an age.

associations.name: Sudden End
associations.significance: The Clovis Comet is tied to the abrupt and catastrophic end of the Clovis culture, symbolizing the fragility of civilization.
12,900 years ago rounds to 13,000, and 13 can represent bad luck or dramatic change in some cultures.

associations.name: The Great Filter
associations.significance: The Clovis Comet event can be seen as a hurdle in civilization's progress, fitting into the Great Filter hypothesis.

associations.name: Black
associations.significance: Black symbolizes destruction, death, and the unknown future, all of which are associated with the Clovis Comet event and its consequences.

companions.name: Phoebe
companions.description: Phoebe, a Titaness associated with prophecy and the moon, acts as a guide, offering insights into the cyclical nature of destruction and rebirth that Clovis Comet represents. She helps Clovis Comet understand the larger cosmic patterns at play.

companions.name: Bifröst
companions.description: Bifröst, the rainbow bridge from Norse mythology, represents the connection between worlds. He assists Clovis Comet by creating pathways across dimensions, enabling the comet to traverse great distances and influence different eras.

companions.name: Yacumama
companions.description: Yacumama, the Amazonian mother of water, attempts to mitigate the damage caused by Clovis Comet's impact. She uses her powers over water to quell fires and nurture life in the aftermath of the comet's passage, symbolizing the resilience of nature.

companions.name: Raven
companions.description: Raven, the trickster figure from various Native American mythologies, follows in the wake of Clovis Comet, scavenging knowledge and power from the destruction. Raven represents the opportunistic nature of chaos and the potential for new beginnings to emerge from devastation.

companions.name: Manu
companions.description: Manu, the progenitor of humanity in Hindu mythology who survived a great flood, aids the comet in reaching its destination. Manu hopes the ensuing destruction will lead to a new, more enlightened age for humanity.
